title: Закон за електронната идентификация
description: Bulgaria's electronic identification legislation supplementing Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 (eIDAS Regulation) with respect to electronic identification schemes, electronic identification means, and the national electronic identification system.

provisions.title: Subject and scope
provisions.content: This Act regulates the conditions and procedure for electronic identification of natural persons and legal persons, the electronic identification scheme of the Republic of Bulgaria, and the requirements for electronic identification means. This Act supplements the directly applicable provisions of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 of the European Parliament and of the Council on electronic identification and trust services for electronic transactions in the internal market.

provisions.title: Definitions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Act: 'electronic identification' means the process of using person identification data in electronic form uniquely representing either a natural or legal person, or a natural person representing a legal person; 'electronic identification means' means a material or immaterial unit containing person identification data and which is used for authentication for an online service; 'authentication' means an electronic process that enables confirmation of the electronic identification of a natural or legal person.

provisions.title: Assurance levels
provisions.content: Electronic identification means shall provide one of the following assurance levels: low — provides a limited degree of confidence in the claimed or asserted identity of a person; substantial — provides a substantial degree of confidence in the claimed or asserted identity of a person; high — provides a higher degree of confidence in the claimed or asserted identity of a person than electronic identification means with the assurance level substantial. The assurance levels shall comply with the specifications set out in Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2015/1502.

provisions.title: National eID scheme
provisions.content: The electronic identification scheme of the Republic of Bulgaria shall be based on the Bulgarian personal identity card containing an electronic identification chip. The scheme shall provide a high level of assurance within the meaning of Article 8(2)(c) of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. The State e-Government Agency shall be responsible for the technical implementation and operation of the national electronic identification infrastructure.

provisions.title: Electronic identification chip
provisions.content: The electronic identification chip embedded in the Bulgarian personal identity card shall contain: the personal identification number (EGN) of the holder; the name of the holder; the date of birth; the nationality; a cryptographic key pair for electronic identification. Access to the identification data shall require the entry of a personal identification number (PIN) by the holder. The chip shall support contactless communication in accordance with applicable technical standards.

provisions.title: Notification to the Commission
provisions.content: The national electronic identification scheme shall be notified to the European Commission in accordance with Article 9 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. The notification shall include: a description of the electronic identification scheme; the applicable assurance level; a description of the entity responsible for the scheme; arrangements for suspension or revocation of the scheme or its compromised parts; liability arrangements.

provisions.title: Mutual recognition
provisions.content: Electronic identification means issued under a notified electronic identification scheme of another Member State of the European Union shall be recognised in the Republic of Bulgaria for cross-border authentication for public services, in accordance with Article 6 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. Online public services requiring electronic identification shall accept electronic identification means with an assurance level equal to or higher than the level required for the service.

provisions.title: Interoperability
provisions.content: The national electronic identification infrastructure shall ensure interoperability with the electronic identification schemes of other Member States through the European interoperability framework established under Article 12 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. The State e-Government Agency shall operate the national eIDAS node ensuring the connection to the eIDAS network for cross-border authentication.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4 - Electronic Identification in the Private Sector
provisions.title: Private sector use
provisions.content: Private sector entities may use the national electronic identification means for the identification and authentication of their customers, subject to the consent of the holder and compliance with the requirements of Regulation (EU) 2016/679. Private sector entities wishing to rely on the national electronic identification scheme shall register with the State e-Government Agency and comply with the technical requirements for integration.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4 - Electronic Identification in the Private Sector
provisions.title: Data protection in electronic identification
provisions.content: The processing of personal data in the context of electronic identification shall comply with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 and the Personal Data Protection Act. The minimum set of person identification data necessary for uniquely representing the natural or legal person shall be processed. The purposes of processing shall be limited to identification and authentication for the specific service requested.

provisions.title: Mobile eID
provisions.content: Electronic identification means may be provided in a mobile form through a qualified application on a mobile device. Mobile electronic identification shall provide an assurance level of at least substantial. The mobile electronic identification application shall implement cryptographic protection of the identification data and shall require biometric or PIN authentication by the holder for each identification transaction.

provisions.title: Security requirements for mobile eID
provisions.content: Mobile electronic identification applications shall meet the following security requirements: secure storage of cryptographic keys in a hardware security module or a secure element of the device; end-to-end encryption of identification data; protection against man-in-the-middle attacks; logging of all identification transactions; automatic revocation in case of device compromise or loss.

provisions.title: Supervisory authority
provisions.content: The State e-Government Agency shall supervise compliance with this Act and the provisions of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 relating to electronic identification. The supervisory authority shall have the power to: conduct inspections of entities relying on the national electronic identification scheme; issue mandatory instructions for remediation of non-compliance; suspend or revoke the registration of private sector entities in case of serious non-compliance.

provisions.title: Administrative sanctions
provisions.content: A private sector entity that uses the national electronic identification means without proper registration shall be subject to a fine of BGN 5,000 to BGN 50,000. A person who misuses electronic identification data or facilitates identity fraud through electronic identification means shall be subject to a fine of BGN 10,000 to BGN 100,000, unless the act constitutes a criminal offence. For repeated violations, fines shall be doubled.

provisions.title: Liability
provisions.content: The State shall be liable for damages caused by the malfunction of the national electronic identification infrastructure or the issuance of an electronic identification means that does not meet the required assurance level. Private sector entities relying on the national electronic identification scheme shall be liable for damages caused by failure to properly verify the electronic identification of their customers.

provisions.title: European Digital Identity Wallet
provisions.content: The Republic of Bulgaria shall ensure the availability of the European Digital Identity Wallet in accordance with the requirements of the amended Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. The European Digital Identity Wallet shall enable users to securely request and obtain, store, select, combine and share electronic identification data and electronic attestations of attributes for the purpose of authentication. The implementation shall comply with the common standards and technical specifications adopted by the European Commission.

definitions.definition: The process of using person identification data in electronic form uniquely representing either a natural or legal person, or a natural person representing a legal person.
A material or immaterial unit containing person identification data and which is used for authentication for an online service.
An electronic process that enables confirmation of the electronic identification of a natural or legal person.
